title: Basic Error Handling: die() and trigger_error()
description: Simple ways to handle errors and generate custom errors.

slides.title: die() and exit()
slides.content:
# die() – Stop and Print Message

If something critical fails, you can stop the script with `die()` or `exit()`. Both output a message and terminate.

```php
$file = fopen('data.txt', 'r') or die('Unable to open file!');
```

This is a quick and dirty way, but not very flexible.

slides.title: trigger_error() – Generate a User Error
trigger_error Example
slides.codeSnippet:
<?php
function divide($a, $b) {
    if ($b == 0) {
        trigger_error('Division by zero', E_USER_ERROR);
        return false;
    }
    return $a / $b;
}
echo divide(10, 0);
?>

quizData.question: What does `die('message')` do?
quizData.explanation: die() prints the message and exits the script.
